text.title: Team Development Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the team-development methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Tuckman stage MUST be diagnosed from observable retro signals (conflict frequency, decision speed, trust score) — not from intuition.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Team charter MUST name decision rights (who decides what) — absence triggers Storming on every contested call.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Every critical skill MUST have ≥2 people marked (primary + secondary) — single-point-of-knowledge is a Storming trigger.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Adding 1 member to a Performing team MUST trigger a 1-week mini-Forming phase — not a full Forming reset.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Retro format MUST rotate quarterly (Start-Stop-Continue, 4Ls, Mad-Sad-Glad, Sailboat) to prevent staleness.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for one-sprint teams, committees, or churn >50%/quarter.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
